text.content: Operator-ruled pilot (2026-07-04): evaluate whether bounded executor work items can round-trip through claude.ai/code cloud sessions with estate lifecycle discipline intact. EXECUTION CONSTRAINT (operator ruling): driven FROM RAMTOP (the PM host) — cloud sessions are launched from the Ramtop side, not orion; the Coverage Debt lane only supplies the guinea-pig items. Scope: pick 2-3 code-only items from WI-1401..1414 (no device, no secrets-sensitive surface); dispatch each to a claude.ai/code session; PR back; run normal review. Deliverable is a FINDINGS NOTE, not the code: lifecycle fit (claim/complete discipline from a cloud session), secrets boundaries (what the cloud session could and could not access, and what it SHOULD not), commit-scope and hook behavior, throughput/latency, and a go/no-go recommendation for standing cloud capacity (the VM question). Time-box: 1-2 days elapsed, not effort.

Time-box: 1-2 days elapsed, not effort.

text.content: AC1: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-celebration.test.tsx covers a profile switch without unmount: the same celebration key shown for profile A must be eligible to show for profile B, while the same-profile rerender remains deduped. AC2: apps/mobile/src/components/feedback/FeedbackProvider.test.tsx or equivalent covers both entry paths: invoking openFeedback through context opens FeedbackSheet, and the useShakeDetector callback opens the same sheet. AC3: apps/mobile/src/lib/profile.test.tsx or a focused guard test compares PROFILE_SCOPED_KEYS against the first segments produced by profile-scoped queryKeys factories and representative scoped literal keys, so a new scoped cache prefix fails until the switch allow-list is updated or explicitly exempted. AC4: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.test.ts or an integration test double-invokes duplicate app/session.completed delivery for the same sessionId and asserts side effects that must be single-fire remain single-fire; the existing opts.idempotency assertion alone is not sufficient. AC5: Run the related Jest targets for all changed tests and report any existing unrelated failures separately.

text.content: WHY: The flow map's S6 gate requires the S3 AccountAdminSheet to cite every rehomed account row before the More tab is deleted; it cites only 5 rows + sign-out and does not even render devices/withdrawal-archive/breakdown-sharing. RISK: More-tab deletion at S6 would silently drop behavior that is only tested in the legacy screens, not at the V2 consolidation point. START: rule WI-1416/D4 first (must the sheet SURFACE the 3 rows, or is transitive reach OK) — that decides whether this is build+test or test-only; then add the missing sheet-row + rare-row-e2e coverage.

text.content:
AC1: Add a red-green-revert regression test in apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-dictation-playback.test.ts proving Speech.speak onError resets non-paused playback from speaking to idle, failing without the onError reset and passing with it.
AC2: Extend apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-dictation-api.test.ts to directly cover useReviewDictation posting to /dictation/review with DICTATION_REVIEW_TIMEOUT_MS signal handling, useRecordDictationResult posting to /dictation/result and invalidating ['dictation-history', activeProfile.id], and useDictationHistory fetching/selecting /dictation/history entries under a profile-scoped key.
AC3: Add a co-located hook test for apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/session/_hooks/use-image-base64.ts covering ready mime resolution, allowlist rejection, read failure, and the 2500ms timeout state without preserving stale refs.
AC4: Add a homework gallery Maestro e2e flow or extend the existing homework camera e2e suite so the gallery-button path is exercised through image selection/preview/use-this, distinct from camera capture and manual-entry paths.
AC5: Verification includes the related mobile Jest tests for the touched hooks/screens and the added/updated homework gallery e2e flow command or documented local-infra reason it could not be run.

text.content: WHY: Current code has several narrow coverage gaps in dictation and homework media flows. The TTS failure reset in use-dictation-playback.ts has no regression test; use-dictation-api.test.ts covers prepare/generate and the review timeout constant but not the useReviewDictation, useRecordDictationResult, or useDictationHistory hooks; use-image-base64.ts has no direct hook test for failed/timeout/allowlist/read-fail behavior; homework camera unit tests cover gallery picker paths, but Maestro homework e2e flows cover manual/camera OCR and permission denial, not gallery entry.

text.content: codex:builder:WI-1411

text.content:
AC1 Recitation: add or extend co-located tests around sessionModeConfig/session route behavior so recitation is included in config/opening-copy coverage and mode=recitation uses the recitation title, subtitle, placeholder, and opening behavior rather than freeform/review fallback.
AC2 Guess Who play integration: add QuizPlayScreen tests for a wrong final free-text Guess Who attempt and a final skip path; assert /check receives answerMode, finalAttempt, and cluesUsed, the server correctAnswer is captured for wrong final attempts, and the completion payload records the final result with answerGiven, correct=false, cluesUsed, and answerMode.
AC3 Dispute payload: add a test that disputes a non-final wrong quiz answer, then finishes or saves the round, and assert completeRound receives disputed:true on that question result.
AC4 History label: add a positive QuizHistoryScreen test for a vocabulary round whose theme contains a supported language prefix; assert the row label includes Vocabulary: <Language>.
AC5 E2E: Save & Finish from the mid-round quit modal has deterministic e2e coverage. Home quiz-discovery e2e is either represented by the existing j26 Playwright spec or equivalent maintained e2e coverage; flow inventory must cite the maintained coverage.
AC6 Docs: update docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md so QUIZ-14 no longer claims None if current code-level coverage proves the difficulty-bump banner; keep QUIZ-05 and QUIZ-16 coverage notes aligned with actual tests.

text.content:
Done when the vocabulary subject screen hides or disables destructive delete affordances for non-owner/proxy viewing states and has a negative screen test mirroring the saved/book/topic write-gating patterns, so a proxy viewer cannot see a delete action that the server will reject.

Book deletion and topic note write/delete surfaces gain negative tests for proxy or impersonated-child viewing states using the established active-profile-role test pattern; the tests assert the write affordance is absent or inert rather than merely asserting a server error after tap.

Progress index coverage proves the requestedProfileId/profileId data-isolation path, parent-proxy routing branch, and ProgressStatsChips isViewingSelf vocabulary privacy control with focused tests.

Verification includes the related mobile Jest test files for touched vocabulary/book/topic/progress surfaces, mobile typecheck if production TSX changes are made, and git diff --check.

text.content: WHY: Library/progress read paths are strong but the write side is under-tested and client gates are inconsistent (vocabulary delete has no client gate at all), and progress data-isolation gates are untested. RISK: a proxy viewer sees delete/write affordances the server rejects (bad UX + no regression guard); a profileId-honor or isViewingSelf privacy gate could regress silently. START: add the missing client gate + negative tests (mirror saved.test.tsx) for vocabulary/book/topic writes, and cover the three progress/index data-isolation gates.

text.content: WHY: The S2 hub's masked-render path is only tested in isolation (canStudy is hardcoded true, so no producer ever masks a proxy viewer), and the assessment surface has no ownership guard test and no test of its pass/fail result UI. RISK: a proxy viewer sees full study/note affordances on the real hub (server blocks the write, but UX/regression is unguarded); the core assessment result screen has no protection. START: add a producer for canStudy + a masked-render integration test; add assessment guard + terminal-result-UI tests.

text.content:
Surface-read evidence: ProfileBasicsStep adult gate and submit path located in apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_components/save-wizard/ProfileBasicsStep.tsx; adjacent adult-gate coverage located in apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.test.tsx; mentor-memory privacy handlers located in apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/mentor-memory.tsx; adjacent hook/prompt coverage located in use-learner-profile and memory-consent prompt tests.

AC1: Add a focused ProfileBasicsStep component regression test for target child or both where the parent birth year resolves under 18: Continue remains disabled, save-basics-adult-required renders, and pressing Continue does not call client.profiles.$post.

AC2: Add self MentorMemoryScreen screen-level tests for memoryConsentStatus pending that press grant and decline and assert useGrantMemoryConsent().mutateAsync receives { consent: 'granted' } and { consent: 'declined' } without childProfileId.

AC3: Add self MentorMemoryScreen screen-level tests for the injection toggle and clear-all path: toggling the saved-notes switch calls useToggleMemoryInjection().mutateAsync with the new value, and confirming clear-all calls useDeleteAllMemory().mutateAsync({}).

AC4: Add or update a save-wizard Maestro flow for a minor-owner rejection path, but mark this as verify-at-e2e-run unless an emulator/dev-client run actually executes it: enter a child/both save target with an under-18 parent birth year, assert save-basics-adult-required is visible, and assert the flow does not advance to save-confirm-land.

rich_text.plain_text:

text.content:
Surface-read evidence: billing-v2 live dispatch and route imports located in apps/api/src/services/billing/billing-v2/index.ts, dispatch.ts, and apps/api/src/routes/billing.ts; quota provisioning located in quota-provision-v2.ts and metering routes/services; family semantics located in family-v2.ts; top-up located in top-up-v2.ts and mobile subscription.tsx; child quota UI located in QuotaExceededCard, SessionMessageActions, use-session-streaming, and session/index.tsx.

AC1: Add real-DB quota-provision-v2 integration coverage for owner and child role resolution, absent profileQuotaUsage provisioning, stale-limit update, and missing/cross-org membership no-provision behavior. Red-green-revert: tests fail if role resolution is changed to legacy/profile-only or insert/update provisioning is removed.

AC2: Add live quota hot-path coverage for per-profile v2 from an absent quota row, preferably through route or metering boundary with only external dependencies stubbed. Assert the row is lazy-provisioned and decremented exactly once, and child 402 details do not expose owner top-up availability. Red-green-revert: test fails if getOrProvisionProfileQuotaUsageV2 is bypassed or per-profile tiers hit shared-pool decrement.

AC3: Add real-DB family-v2 integration coverage for current service semantics: list, pool status, add validation of an existing same-org profile, and remove/archive/revoke behavior. Cover family/pro happy path, plus/free rejection, over-cap rejection, cross-org rejection, owner-removal rejection, and archived members excluded from count/list. Red-green-revert: tests fail if archived persons remain billable/listed or owner/cross-org removal succeeds.

text.content:
AC4: Add mobile child in-chat quota coverage at code level and e2e level. Unit/screen coverage asserts sessionIsOwner=false renders child actions, hides upgrade/top-up actions, and disables composer after structured 402. Maestro child-profile flow asserts quota-exceeded-card, quota-notify-parent-btn, quota-go-home-btn, and no quota-upgrade-btn; mark device assertions verify-at-e2e-run unless actually run.

AC5: Add mobile top-up success-to-poll screen test. Paid tier plus top-up package plus successful purchase enters polling UI, refetches usage, confirms only when topUpCreditsRemaining > baseCredits, shows success alert, and clears in-flight state. Red-green-revert: test fails if polling is skipped or confirmation uses >= baseCredits.

AC6: Keep live RevenueCat purchase e2e separate from headless code claims. A headless executor may add or adjust YAML/seeded confirmed-state tests, but live store purchase and RevenueCat sandbox confirmation remain verify-at-e2e-run unless executed on a configured device.

text.content:
Done when apps/api/src/routes/visibility.test.ts or the locally preferred co-located route test covers all six routes from apps/api/src/routes/visibility.ts: POST /visibility/links, POST /visibility/links/:id/accept, POST /visibility/links/:id/revoke, GET /visibility/links/:id/contract, POST /visibility/reports/:personId/appeal, and GET /visibility/reports/:personId/shared-record.

The tests assert route-boundary behavior, not service internals: invalid UUID path params return HTTP 400 before the corresponding service is called; invalid JSON bodies for the three JSON-body routes return HTTP 400 before the corresponding service is called; caller/profile context failures remain explicit HTTP errors rather than falling through to service work.

The test follows existing apps/api/src/routes conventions: mount a small Hono app with seeded context, keep any boundary doubles narrowly scoped and justified under the repo GC1/GC6 policy, and verify with the focused API Jest command for the touched route test.

text.content: WHY: The supporter artifact-wall on /now (the highest-traffic supporter read surface) drops parked_item/ledger_moment for supporters, but has no negative-path test — unlike supporter-structural-mask.ts which is well covered. RISK: a regression could leak child free-text/transcript-adjacent content to a supporter feed undetected. START: seed a real supportership edge + child ledger/parked data and assert /now?scope=person and ?scope=supporter-hub exclude it, plus a no-edge -> 403 case.

No scoped flow remains counted as live coverage while asserting removed copy/testIDs or a placeholder/deferred surface.
AC2: Parent dashboard flows no longer assert the removed Tonight / Conversation starters copy; their required assertions target current parent-home behavior from ParentHomeScreen/en.json using stable testIDs or current copy, without weakening to a generic screen-visible check.
AC3: post-auth-comprehensive-devclient.yaml is either rewritten against current post-auth surfaces or removed/demoted from live coverage with an in-repo rationale and inventory update. It must not hard-tap removed theme/persona controls such as switch-to-teen or the deleted (parent) route group.
AC4: parent/consent-management.yaml no longer waits for grace-period-banner after withdrawal from the child detail screen. It asserts the current consent-withdrawn-empty-state/restore path or is retired in favor of named unit/API coverage, with any docs/inventory claim updated accordingly.
AC5: retention/library.yaml is reconciled with current Library v3 behavior: either it is relabeled as Library navigation coverage, or it adds a real current retention signal assertion from the shelf/list/topic surface. Docs/inventory no longer claim retention-pill coverage that the YAML does not prove.
AC6: Verification includes a grep/static check that the removed literals/testIDs from this WI are absent from live scoped YAML assertions, plus syntax validation and emulator execution for repaired Maestro flows. If a flow is retired instead of repaired, the replacemen
